title: Sum and Difference & Multiple angle Formula

concepts.title: Sum and difference formulas for sin,cos,tan and cot
concepts.conceptBody:
Sum and difference formulas 

  • $\text{ }\!\!~\!\!\text{ sin}\left( \text{A}+\text{B} \right)=\text{sinAcosB}+\text{sinBcosA}$
  • $\text{ }\!\!~\!\!\text{ sin}\left( \text{A}-\text{B} \right)=\text{sinAcosB}-\text{sinBcosA}$
  • $\text{ }\!\!~\!\!\text{ cos}\left( \text{A}+\text{B} \right)=\text{cosAcosB}-\text{sinAsinB}$
  • $\text{ }\!\!~\!\!\text{ cos}\left( \text{A}-\text{B} \right)=\text{cosAcosB}+\text{sinAsinB}$

Note the cos formulas, they have (-) sign for sum formula and (+) ve sign for difference

  • $\text{ }\!\!~\!\!\text{ tan}\left( \text{A}+\text{B} \right)=\frac{\text{tanA}+\text{tanB}}{1-\text{tanAtanB}}$
  • $\text{tan}\left( \text{A}-\text{B} \right)=\frac{\text{tanA}-\text{tanB}}{1+\text{tanAtanB}}$

where $\text{A},\text{B}\ne \left( \text{n}+\frac{1}{2} \right)\pi $

  • $\text{ }\!\!~\!\!\text{ cot}\left( \text{A}+\text{B} \right)=\frac{\text{cotAcotB}-1}{\text{cotA}+\text{cotB}}$
  • $\text{ }\!\!~\!\!\text{ cot}\left( \text{A}-\text{B} \right)=\frac{\text{cotAcotB}+1}{\text{cotB}-\text{cotA}}$

where $A,B\ne n\pi $

Cot formula's are opposite of tan formulas, the denominator & numerator part are switched.

concepts.title: Important deductions
concepts.conceptBody:
Some Important deduction 

  • $\text{ }\!\!~\!\!\text{ sin}\left( \text{A}+\text{B} \right)\text{sin}\left( \text{A}-\text{B} \right)=\text{si}{{\text{n}}^{2}}\text{ }\!\!~\!\!\text{ A}-\text{si}{{\text{n}}^{2}}\text{ }\!\!~\!\!\text{ B}\,\,={{\cos }^{2}}B-{{\cos }^{2}}A$
  • $\begin{align} & \cos (A+B)\cos (A-B)={{\cos }^{2}}A-{{\sin }^{2}}B \\ & \,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,={{\cos }^{2}}B-{{\sin }^{2}}A \\ \end{align}$
  • $\text{tan}A\pm \text{tan}B=\frac{\text{sin}\left( A\pm B \right)}{\text{cos}A\text{cos}B}$
  • $\text{ }\!\!~\!\!\text{ cot}A\pm \text{cot}B=\frac{\text{sin}\left( B\pm A \right)}{\text{sin}A\text{sin}B}$
  • $\text{tan}\left( \frac{\pi }{4}+\theta \right)=\frac{1+\text{tan}\theta }{1-\text{tan}\theta }\And \text{tan}\left( \frac{\pi }{4}-\theta \right)=\frac{1-\text{tan}\theta }{1+\text{tan}\theta }$

This formula will be used in various number of questions.
